content:
# Igneous and Metamorphic Petrology

**Summary**: The study of the origin, composition, and structure of rocks.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize mantle xenoliths.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize A-type and I-type granitoids.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize the Al-Ghurayyah pluton.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] ['mineral chemistry', 'geothermobarometry', 'metasomatism']

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize A-type and I-type granitoids.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] applied igneous-metamorphic-petrology to characterize the Al-Ghurayyah pluton.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] ['petrology', 'trace-element-geochemistry']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Harrat Uwayrid peridotite xenoliths are similar to other western Arabian peridotites. ([[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023, p. 90]])

- Granitoid suites are divided into three groups based on age: 760-660 Ma, 660-610 Ma, and 610-565 Ma. ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 21]])

- The pluton is a highly fractionated Fe-rich metaluminous to peraluminous A-type granite. ([[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024, p. 15]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] Determining the degree of melting and oxidation state.
